_comment: Example replay_filter cuts that SPLIT one DAQ run into two output files at a target-state transition — the empty-target / full-target case where the cell is emptied or filled mid-run without stopping the DAQ. The 'split' block names the slow-control PV to watch (the target cell pressure, TGT:PRad:Cell_P) and how to find the transition; replay_filter then writes <out>_<labelA>.root (before the transition) and <out>_<labelB>.root (after it), each with its own .report.json and live-charge integral. The split runs on the already-replayed ROOT, so you replay the full run once and split in seconds — no second replay. The other blocks (livetime/epics/charge) are the usual quality cuts and apply to BOTH sides; delete them for a pure split with no quality filtering. Set 'threshold' to a value between your empty and full Cell_P readings (or use 'level_low'/'level_high' for a hysteresis band, robust to a noisy PV). The guard band drops slow-control checkpoints around the crossing so events taken while the cell is still ramping land in NEITHER output: 'checkpoints' mode drops +/- N points (checkpoints are ~regularly spaced, so this is effectively a time guard); 'stability' mode instead extends the guard until the PV has settled within 'epsilon' of each side's level. labels[0] is the before-transition side, labels[1] the after.

split._hysteresis_alternative: Replace 'threshold' with an explicit band when the PV is noisy near the crossing — the transition latches only when the reading reaches the far level: "level_low": 0.2, "level_high": 2.0
split._stability_alternative: Replace the guard with: "guard": { "mode": "stability", "epsilon": 0.1, "consecutive": 3 } to drop the ramp by settle-band instead of a fixed point count.
